statement.en: Assessment of the Environmental Impacts of Landslides on Groundwater Resources Using Geoelectrical Surveys and Dipole-Dipole Arrays
statement.summary: The present study aimed to assess the geological impacts of landslides on groundwater resources along the Shemshak-Dizin road, Iran, using the geoelectric geophysical method based on a dipole-dipole array. Thus, five geoelectric acquisition profiles were designed and implemented to identify the landslide geometry and assess its impacts on the regional groundwater system. The results of reverse modeling showed the clear boundary of the landslide layers and the areas with high humidity, as well as the lithological facies and the local water infiltration through fractures downstream of the landslide. The comparative results of the quantity of groundwater at present with those of the previous year show that the groundwater level in this area has decreased by an average of 14 meters compared to

proposer: Pejman Aramfar, Arezou Dorostian, Soheila Bouzari et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a field-applied geophysical study (2026 preprint) using established methods (dipole-dipole geoelectrical surveys) to assess real-world environmental phenomena. The methodology is sound and replicable, but the study appears incomplete (abstract truncated) and the specific claims about 14m groundwater decline require peer validation. Not yet formally published/validated.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 地滑りが地下水系を破壊する様子を、抵抗率の深さ依存と断層領域での乱れで直感する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 破砕された層を貫く電流パターンが、隠れた水の動きと断層の幾何学を明かす。
